text:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Okonkwo is a 63-year-old female with a newly diagnosed large right renal mass measuring 11.4 cm with heterogeneous enhancement and extension into the right renal vein, retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and bilateral pulmonary nodules identified on recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis. The overall constellation of findings is consistent with stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma. This MRI brain is obtained as part of the systemic staging evaluation prior to planned cytoreductive surgery and initiation of systemic therapy. History of type 2 diabetes and hypertension. Baseline creatinine 1.3 mg/dL (eGFR 74 mL/min/1.73m2). Gadolinium administered with renal function review documented.

TECHNIQUE:
Multisequence MRI of the brain performed on a 3.0 Tesla scanner without and with intravenous gadolinium-based contrast agent (gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg). Sequences obtained include: sagittal T1-weighted localizer; axial T2-weighted fast spin-echo; axial FLAIR (fluid-attenuated inversion recovery); axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping; axial gradient recalled echo (GRE) susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI); pre- and post-contrast axial T1-weighted spin-echo; post-contrast coronal and sagittal T1-weighted sequences. Slice thickness 5 mm for standard sequences; 1 mm isotropic 3D post-contrast T1 MPRAGE acquired and reformatted in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes for high-resolution evaluation of parenchymal and leptomeningeal enhancement. No prior brain MRI available for comparison.

FINDINGS:

SUPRATENTORIAL STRUCTURES:

Cerebral Cortex and Subcortical White Matter: No focal cortical or subcortical enhancing lesion is identified. No mass, mass effect, or sulcal effacement. No areas of restricted diffusion to suggest acute or subacute ischemic infarction. No hemorrhagic foci on susceptibility-weighted imaging. No cortical or subcortical nodules concerning for metastatic deposits.

Periventricular and Deep White Matter: There are scattered, punctate to small, confluent foci of T2/FLAIR hyperintensity in the subcortical and periventricular white matter of both cerebral hemispheres, most prominent in the bilateral frontal and parietal regions, with a few additional foci in the posterior parietal white matter bilaterally. These do not demonstrate corresponding enhancement, restricted diffusion, mass effect, or blooming on susceptibility sequences. The distribution and imaging characteristics are consistent with mild-to-moderate chronic small vessel ischemic changes (Fazekas grade 2), which are not unexpected in a patient of this age with long-standing hypertension and type 2 diabetes. No perilesional edema. No leptomeningeal involvement.

Basal Ganglia, Thalami, Internal Capsule, and Deep Gray Matter: Unremarkable. No signal abnormality, enhancement, or mass identified within the caudate nuclei, putamina, globi pallidi, thalami, or internal capsule.

Ventricles and Cerebrospinal Fluid Spaces: The lateral ventricles, third ventricle, and fourth ventricle are of normal caliber and morphology. No hydrocephalus. The sulci and cisterns are patent and of appropriate size for age. No leptomeningeal or ependymal enhancement.

Corpus Callosum: Intact throughout its length. No signal abnormality.

Cerebral Midline: Midline structures are in normal position. No shift. No subfalcine, uncal, or transtentorial herniation.

INFRATENTORIAL STRUCTURES:

Cerebellum: The cerebellar hemispheres and vermis appear normal in signal intensity, morphology, and size. No focal enhancing lesion, mass, or signal abnormality. No evidence of metastatic involvement of the posterior fossa parenchyma. No restricted diffusion.

Brainstem: The midbrain, pons, and medulla are normal in caliber and signal. No intrinsic or extrinsic lesion identified. No focal enhancement.

Cerebellopontine Angle Cisterns and Internal Auditory Canals: Symmetric. No mass, enhancement, or cranial nerve abnormality. No acoustic neuroma or other extra-axial lesion in this region.

CEREBROVASCULAR STRUCTURES:
No arteriovenous malformation or cavernous malformation identified. No abnormal vascular flow voids. No evidence of dural sinus thrombosis on available sequences.

EXTRAXIAL COMPARTMENTS:
No subdural or epidural collections. The dura is unremarkable. No pachymeningeal or leptomeningeal enhancement to suggest carcinomatous meningitis. The calvarium demonstrates normal T1 marrow signal throughout — no focal marrow-replacing or marrow-infiltrating lesion to suggest osseous metastatic involvement of the skull.

SELLA AND PARASELLAR REGION:
The pituitary gland is of normal size and morphology without focal signal abnormality or mass effect on the optic chiasm. No pituitary adenoma identified. The optic nerves and chiasm appear normal in thickness and signal.

ORBITS (PARTIALLY VISUALIZED):
The globes appear normal bilaterally. No intraorbital mass or abnormal enhancement on the partially visualized portions of the orbits.

SINONASAL STRUCTURES (PARTIALLY VISUALIZED):
Minimal mucosal thickening in the left maxillary sinus, likely reflecting chronic inflammatory/retention change. This is incidental and of no immediate clinical consequence.

POST-CONTRAST ASSESSMENT:
Following administration of gadolinium, no parenchymal, leptomeningeal, or pachymeningeal enhancing focus is identified. No enhancing nodule or ring-enhancing lesion to suggest metastatic deposit, primary neoplasm, or abscess. No abnormal vascular enhancement pattern. Enhancement of normal structures including choroid plexus and dural sinuses appears physiologic.

IMPRESSION:

1. No intracranial metastatic disease. No parenchymal, leptomeningeal, or osseous enhancing lesion to suggest brain involvement by the known renal cell carcinoma.

2. Mild-to-moderate white matter signal changes in the bilateral cerebral hemispheres, consistent with chronic small vessel ischemic disease in the setting of long-standing hypertension and diabetes mellitus. These findings are nonprogressive in nature based on their morphology and distribution, and are not related to the patient's known malignancy.

3. No acute intracranial process. No evidence of infarction, hemorrhage, hydrocephalus, or herniation.

4. Minimal left maxillary sinus mucosal thickening, likely of inflammatory/retention etiology, incidental.

RADIOLOGIST ATTESTATION:
Images personally reviewed. Report finalized by attending neuroradiologist.
